
1.marx is a PhD dissertation in philosophy. It analyzes the philosophies of Marx and Heidegger and suggests what each could learn from the other. You are a philosophy teacher summarizing the arguments of this dissertation for graduate students studying philosophy. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each chapter analyzing its main arguments. Then write an approximately 500-word-long summary of the entire dissertation, based on the chapter summaries. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

10.science is a collection of essays about the scientific structure of CSCL. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each chapter analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

11.theory is a collection of essays about a proposed theory of thinking by online teams of students called "group cognition". You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each chapter analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

12.math is a collection of essays on issues of CSCL software for learning mathematics. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each investigation analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

13.dynamic is a collection of essays about ways of analyzing students learning dynamic geometry together using CSCL software. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each chapter analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

14.topics is a collection of four curricula for learning dynamic geometry together using CSCL software. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each chapter analyzing its main features and recommendation. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how the four main sections relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

15.global is a collection of versions of the foundational paper for computer-supported collaborative learning.  You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this paper for advanced graduate students studying learning science,  social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of the first chapter from this book, analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write a sentence for each of the other chapters, stating that it is an earlier version or a translation into another language. Then write an approximately 100-word-long overview of the entire book, based on the summaries of its chapters and stating that the book is an attempt to reach a global audience. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.  Produce a txt report listing the chapter and book titles followed by their summaries. Also list 5 to 8 keywwords for the first chapter You can access the full text of the book and each of its chapters in the elibrary_cache.pkl . 

16.ijcscl is a collection of editorial introductions to each of the isssues of the International Journal of CSCL during its first decade of publication (2006-2016).  You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for advanced graduate students studying learning science,  social science and computer science. Write an approximately 100-word-long summary of each chapter from this book, analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 300-word-long overview of the entire book, based on the summaries of its chapters and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.  Produce a txt report listing the chapter and book titles followed by their summaries. Also list 5 to 8 keywwords for each chapter You can access the full text of the book and each of its chapters in the elibrary_cache.pkl . 

17.proposals, is a collection of grant proposals by the author, primarily to support his CSCL research.  You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for advanced graduate students studying learning science,  social science and computer science. Write an approximately 200-word-long summary of each chapter from this book, analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 400-word-long overview of the entire book, based on the summaries of its chapters and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.  Produce a txt report listing the chapter and book titles followed by their summaries. Also list 5 to 8 keywwords for each chapter You can access the full text of the book and each of its chapters in the elibrary_cache.pkl . 

18.overview is a collection of sections providing overviews of the e-library collection of texts and of overview or incidents from the life of the author of those books.  Write an approximately 100-word-long summary of each chapter from this book, analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 300-word-long overview of the entire book, based on the summaries of its chapters and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.  Produce a txt report listing the chapter and book titles followed by their summaries. Also list 5 to 8 keywwords for each chapter You can access the full text of the book and each of its chapters in the elibrary_cache.pkl . 

19.investigations is a collection of theoretical investigations related to the foundations of group cognition theory. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each investigation analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

2.tacit is a PhD dissertation in computer science discussing the design of software for supporting lunar habitat design based on architectural design theory and hermeneutic philosophy. You are a computer science teacher summarizing the arguments of this dissertation for graduate students studying computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each chapter analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire dissertation, based on the chapter summaries. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

20.form is a collection of sections presenting the work of the author in different fields of 3-D art.  Write an approximately 100-word-long summary of each chapter from this book, analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 200-word-long overview of the entire book, based on the summaries of its chapters and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.  Produce a txt report listing the chapter and book titles followed by their summaries. Also list 5 to 8 keywwords for each chapter You can access the full text of the book and each of its chapters in the elibrary_cache.pkl . 

21.game is a collection of sections presenting challenges for online teams of students learning the basics of dynamic geometry.  Write an approximately 50-word-long summary of each chapter from this book, analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 200-word-long overview of the entire book, based on the summaries of its chapters and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.  Produce a txt report listing the chapter and book titles followed by their summaries. Also list 5 to 8 keywwords for each chapter You can access the full text of the book and each of its chapters in the elibrary_cache.pkl 

22.climate is a collection of chapters concerning the effects of climate change and sea level rise on the town of Chatham, MA, and its salt mrashes.  You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying environmental science and public policy. Write an approximately 200-word-long summary of each chapter from this book, analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 300-word-long overview of the entire book, based on the summaries of its chapters and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.  Produce a txt report listing the chapter and book titles followed by their summaries. Also list 5 to 8 keywwords for each chapter You can access the full text of the book and each of its chapters in the elibrary_cache.pkl 

3.gc is a set of papers on topics of Design of Computer Support for Collaboration, Analysis of Collaborative Knowledge Building and Theory of Group Cognition. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying learning science, social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each chapter analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

4.svmt is a collection of papers studying  teams of students learning in an online environment called VMT. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying learning science,  social science and computer science. Write an approximately 200-word-long summary of each chapter of 4.svmt in the cache, analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book. Also list 5 to 8 keywwords for each chapter.

5.euclid is a set of papers analyzing an online environment called VMT. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each chapter analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

6.analysis is a set of papers studying  three students learning in an online environment called VMT. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each chapter analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

7.philosophy is a collection of essays on topics of social philosophy. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying philosophy. Write an approximately 200-word-long summary of each essay analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 400-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book. Produce a txt report listing the chapter and book titles followed by their summaries. Also list 5 to 8 keywwords for each chapter You can access the full text of the book and each of its chapters in the elibrary_cache.pkl . 

8.software is a collection of essays on how to personalize software. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each investigation analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.

9.cscl is a collection of essays on cscl research. You are a teacher summarizing the arguments of this collection for graduate students studying social science and computer science. Write an approximately 300-word-long summary of each investigation analyzing its main concerns, arguments, methods and findings. Then write an approximately 500-word-long overview of the entire collection, based on the chapter summaries and suggesting how some of the essays relate to each other. Produce a txt report listing the chapter (or book) titles followed by their summaries. Do not use names or terminology that do not appear in the book.
